title: The Tower Treasure
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: The Hardy Boys' first mystery begins when the town of Bayport is rocked by a series of daring robberies. Frank and Joe Hardy follow the clues to a startling discovery hidden in an old tower.

chapters.title: 24 - The Last of the Tower Case

title: The House On The Cliff
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: Frank and Joe Hardy investigate a mysterious house on the cliff that is rumored to be haunted — but something far more dangerous is hiding inside.

title: The Secret of the Old Mill
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: Counterfeit bills are flooding Bayport, and the trail leads Frank and Joe Hardy to a creepy old mill on the outskirts of town.

title: The Missing Chums
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: When two of the Hardy Boys' friends go missing during a boating trip, Frank and Joe race against time to rescue them from dangerous criminals.

title: Hunting For Hidden Gold
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: The Hardy Boys head to the Montana wilderness chasing a legendary cache of stolen gold — and find themselves in the crosshairs of ruthless criminals.

chapters.title: 13 - In The Depths Of The Earth

title: The Shore Road Mystery
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: Cars are vanishing along Shore Road, and Frank and Joe Hardy go undercover to crack the car theft ring operating right in their backyard.

title: The Secret of the Caves
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: A mysterious recluse, a series of strange signals, and a network of sea caves all point to a dangerous secret Frank and Joe must uncover.

title: The Mystery of Cabin Island
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: Stranded on a snow-covered island in winter, the Hardy Boys discover the island holds a secret that someone will kill to protect.

title: Great Airport Mystery
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: A series of thefts at the Bayport Airport puts Frank and Joe Hardy on the trail of a smuggling ring using small planes for their criminal operations.

chapters.title: 15 - On the Trail of Ollie Jacobs

title: Ted Scott's Daring Long-Distance Flight
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: Young aviator Ted Scott makes an audacious solo flight across the Atlantic in this exciting series opener, inspired by the era of daring pioneer pilots.

title: Rescued In The Clouds
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: Ted Scott takes to the skies again in a thrilling rescue mission, battling storms and danger high above the clouds.

title: Lost In the Wilderness
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description: Ted Scott crashes in a remote wilderness and must fight for survival while enemies close in around him.

title: Danger at the Drawbridge
author: Mildred A. Wirt
description: Ace girl reporter Penny Parker investigates sinister goings-on at an old drawbridge that holds the key to a dangerous mystery.

title: The Clue of the Silken Ladder
author: Mildred A. Wirt
description: Penny Parker follows a trail of clues that leads to a secret society, a stolen jewel, and a silken ladder left at the scene of a crime.

title: The Secret Pact
author: Mildred A. Wirt
description: Penny Parker uncovers a secret underground network and a pact that endangers everyone she loves.

title: The Clock Strikes Thirteen
author: Mildred A. Wirt
description: When a mysterious clock strikes thirteen, Penny Parker is plunged into a puzzling mystery involving a cursed manor and its strange inhabitants.

title: The Wishing Well
author: Mildred A. Wirt
description: Penny Parker investigates a wishing well with a dark history — and discovers that someone is using old legends to cover up very modern crimes.

title: The Mercer Boys' Cruise in the Lassie
author: Capwell Wyckoff
description: The Mercer Boys set off on a summer cruise aboard their boat the Lassie — only to find themselves entangled in a mystery that threatens their lives.

title: Alone in London
author: Hesba Stretton
description: A touching and moving story of a young girl navigating life alone in the bustling streets of London.

chapters.title: 9 - A New Broom and a Crossing

title: Toby Tyler; or, Ten Weeks with a Circus
author: James Otis
description: Toby Tyler runs away from home to join the circus, where he meets new friends, faces hardships, and learns the true meaning of home.

chapters.title: 4 - The First Day With the Circus
13 - Toby Attempts to Resign His Situation
14 - Mr. Castle Teaches Toby to Ride
15 - Toby's Friends Present Him with a Costume
16 - Toby's First Appearance in the Ring
19 - Mr. Stubbs's Mischief, and His Sad Fate

title: The Prince and the Pauper
author: Mark Twain
description: Mark Twain's beloved tale of two boys who look identical — one a prince, one a pauper — who switch places and discover the truth about wealth, power, and justice.

chapters.title: 1 - The Birth of the Prince and the Pauper

title: George Washington: The Making of America's First President
author: John Abbott
description: A compelling audiobook biography of George Washington tracing his extraordinary journey from Virginia planter to the first President of the United States.

chapters.title: 4 - The Warrior, the Statesman, and the Planter
11 - The Loss of Philadelphia, and the Capture of Burgoyne

title: In Freedom's Cause
author: G.A. Henty
description: G.A. Henty's thrilling historical adventure following a young Scotsman who fights alongside William Wallace and Robert the Bruce for Scottish independence.

title: The Great Fire of London
author: G.A. Henty
description: G.A. Henty's gripping historical adventure set against the backdrop of one of the most devastating fires in London's history.

chapters.title: 10 - How John Wilkes Fought the Dutch

title: A Millionaire at Sixteen — Kidnapped at Sea!
author: Various
description: A young man's fortunes are upended when he is kidnapped at sea in this rousing multi-voice adventure audiobook.

chapters.title: 4 - The Party that went to Southfield
9 - A Dinner in the Second Cabin
12 - The Possessor of a Mighty Secret
16 - Allegiance to the Powers that be
34 - A Wreck on the Bermuda Reef

title: The World's Greatest Military Spies and Secret Service Agents
author: George Barton
description: Dramatic true accounts of history's most daring spies and secret agents, brought to life in this compelling audiobook by George Barton.

chapters.title: 1 - The Alsatian Smuggler Who Helped Napoleon Capture the Austrian Army
2 - Belle Boyd, the Confederate Girl Who Saved Stonewall Jackson
3 - The Indian Scout Who Was the Hero of King Philip’s War
4 - How the Suicide of a Staff Officer Averted War Between Austria and Russia
5 - The Romantic Side of Major André’s Unsuccessful Exploit
6 - The Strange Adventures of the Chevalier D’Eon at the Russian Court
7 - How Napoleon’s Chief Spy Hoodwinked the Emperor of Austria
8 - Lydia Darrah, the Brave Quakeress Who Saved Washington’s Army
9 - Doctor Steiber and the Mystery of the Franco-Prussian War
10 - The Adventure of Private Morgan in the Camp of Cornwallis
11 - The Mysterious and Romantic Case of General Napper Tandy
12 - How Monsieur de Meinau Helped Make Jerome Bonaparte King of Westphalia
13 - The Strange Mystery Surrounding the Betrayal of Captain Nathan Hale
14 - Major Le Caron and the Fenian Invasion of Canada
15 - How Emma Edmonds Penetrated the Confederate Lines
16 - The Amazing Adventure of Brigadier-General Lafayette C. Baker
17 - The Mysterious “F” and the Captured Troopers
18 - The Mysterious Man Who Asked for a Light
19 - Carl Lody and Spies of the World’s Greatest War

title: Antoine of Oregon: A Story of the Oregon Trail
author: James Otis
description: A vivid frontier adventure following young Antoine on the legendary Oregon Trail, facing hardships and wonders of the American West.

title: Brock: The Hero of Upper Canada
author: Thomas Marquis
description: The story of General Isaac Brock, the British commander who became a legendary hero during the War of 1812 defending Upper Canada.

title: In Ship and Prison
author: Various
description: A gripping account of five years in the Continental Navy with Captain Samuel Tucker during the American Revolutionary War.
